name: creative-writing
description: Iterative fiction writing evaluated for voice, sensory precision, and narrative control.

scenarios.name: The Last Ferry Opening
scenarios.description: Draft and refine an opening that establishes a distinctive voice, place, and narrative question.
scenarios.prompt: Write the opening 500–700 words of a literary mystery. On the final ferry to a storm-isolated island, a burned-out marine biologist recognizes a suitcase that belonged to her brother, who disappeared twelve years ago. Establish a distinctive first-person voice, make the ferry and weather tangible, and end on a discovery that forces her to act. Do not explain the brother's disappearance yet.
scenarios.participantPersona: a discerning developmental editor who responds to the draft and requests one concrete, high-leverage revision each round

criteria.name: voice
criteria.description: The narrator has a specific, consistent perspective with language that feels authored rather than generic.

criteria.name: imagery
criteria.description: Sensory details make the ferry, storm, and emotional tension vivid without overcrowding the prose.

criteria.name: coherence
criteria.description: Character motivation, pacing, clues, and revisions form a clear opening arc with a compelling final turn.
